selected_important_turn.text: Would you be willing to take more firewood for less food?
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first concrete integrative proposal that shifts the conversation from information exchange to trade-offs. It opens the possibility of a mutually beneficial allocation of food and firewood and sets the tone for cooperation.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It asks a general willingness to trade but doesn't specify exact terms, tradeoffs, or any compensating offers for water. That vagueness hands the other side the initiative to fill in details (which later leads them to propose a self-favoring water split). Because it comes early, a stronger, more complete proposal here could have shaped the whole division more equitably.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Be more specific and package the trade: propose concrete quantities or a tentative full split (including water) or tie the concession on food/firewood to a concrete concession on water or another benefit. Also briefly explain why the trade is fair (e.g., both keep most of their top priority) to make acceptance more likely.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (clearer, richer trade package makes integrative gains easier)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (less chance of opponent filling gaps with one-sided demands)

value2reason.Low: Clean water is an important resource and something you cannot go without for long.
value2reason.Medium: I don't know how to start a fire without firewood.
value2reason.High: I am not an outdoorsy person. I would have a hard time identifying and finding food on my own.

value2reason.Low: I enjoy hiking a great deal and water is an essential item to hike at my best.
value2reason.Medium: It is supposed to be a very cold evening and firewood would be helpful to stay warm.
value2reason.High: I didn't have a chance to eat before, so would like to have some food packages so I am not hungry during the trip.

dialogue.text: What are you most interested in getting?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think food is my highest priority, What about you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Food is also my highest priority, but firewood is also pretty close for me.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Would you be willing to take more firewood for less food?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I propose we split the supplies as follows: 2 Food, 1 Firewood, and 0 Water for me, and 1 Food, 2 Firewood, and 3 Water for you. This way, I get most of my high-priority Food, and you get more Water, which is essential for both of us. Let me know if this works for you.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm not sure about the 3 Water for me. I value Food and Firewood more. How about we adjust to 1 Food, 1 Firewood, and 2 Water for me, and 2 Food, 2 Firewood, and 1 Water for you? That way we both get what we need better.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I see your point. Let's try 2 Food, 1 Firewood, and 1 Water for me, and 1 Food, 2 Firewood, 2 Water for you. This balances our priorities—more Food for me, more Firewood for you, and fair Water sharing. Does this work?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think this split makes sense. Let me confirm: 2 Food, 1 Firewood, and 1 Water for you, and 1 Food, 2 Firewood, and 2 Water for me? That aligns with our priorities and ensures all packages are distributed.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Yes, that's correct. I'll take 2 Food, 1 Firewood, and 1 Water. You get 1 Food, 2 Firewood, and 2 Water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Deal confirmed: I take 2 Food, 1 Firewood, and 1 Water. You get 1 Food, 2 Firewood, and 2 Water. All packages distributed.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
